# Auburn Septic — Low-Risk Application Plan

**Status:** Planning only. Do not edit Auburn broadly from Springfield experiments.

## Protected Role

Auburn remains the reference implementation. Preserve its architecture, live citation history, backlink logs, linkable-asset work, renter-readiness records, and existing guardrails.

## Apply Only After Springfield Validation

| Module | Auburn action | Limit |
|---|---|---|
| Citation intelligence | Map current citations into the expanded qualification fields | Audit consistency; do not resubmit live listings |
| Competitor citation gaps | Refresh legitimate septic/local competitor gaps | Read-only; dedupe against existing batch |
| Entity eligibility | Check NAP, operator/renter dependencies, and evidence gaps | No identity change without specific approval |
| GBP prevention/recovery | Add future legitimate-business SOP reference | No profile edits or recovery action |
| Off-page authority | Continue conservative competitor replication and local/septic resources | Avoid paid lead platforms and spam already rejected |
| Linkable assets | Reuse existing Placer County homeowner checklist system | Do not create overlapping assets |
| Renter readiness | Add lead-quality/economics/attribution fields | Preserve existing trial and flat-fee strategy |

## Auburn Audit Queue

1. Citation NAP and live-URL verification.
2. Duplicate/conflict and account-ownership review.
3. Remaining legitimate septic-industry and local gaps.
4. Backlink register normalization using the shared scorecard.
5. Renter-dependent queue and lead-quality ledger alignment.
6. GBP eligibility/prevention/recovery documentation for a legitimate operator only.

## Stop Conditions

No core architecture, production claim, citation, GBP, routing, renter contact, outreach, spend, or listing change without evidence of a specific issue and explicit approval.
